trust_boundary.reliance_statement: This adapter describes a simulated policy engine decision shape. The engine is an evidence source, not a ProofRail trust authority.

control_surface.description: Simulated policy engine emitting decision events for protected actions evaluated against declared policies.

decision_event.description: Simulated policy engine emits a structured decision record per evaluated protected action.

bypass_evidence.limitation: A policy engine only sees evaluations submitted to it. Out-of-band actions are not observable here; bypass evidence should come from a gateway or SIEM.

revocation_status.description: Policy engine records the authority/policy snapshot identifier used at decision time, enabling revocation linkage.

subject_hashes.description: Policy engine can expose SHA-256 of the policy bundle and decision payload.

source_identity.description: Source identity is the policy decision point identity declared in adapter metadata; this is not a trust decision.

timestamp_integrity.description: Decision records carry engine timestamps; v0.2.6 does not verify clock trust.

normalization_notes: Maps policy effects (permit/deny) to ProofRail allow/deny-style decision observations.
Maps policy rationale strings to the normalized reason field unchanged.
Does not assert that the policy bundle itself is correct or up to date.

adapter_limitations: Static descriptor only; no real policy engine integration.
Policy engine only observes evaluations submitted to it; it cannot prove no out-of-band invocations occurred.
Does not assert that any declared policy is sufficient for the relying-party's purposes.
non_claims: This adapter is not a certification.
This adapter does not make the policy engine a trust authority.
This adapter does not assert that any real policy engine product is conformant.
